base_rows.story:
The sun came out bright and warm on the beach.  
A little boy sat on the sand, watching the waves roll in.  
He found a red hat by the shore and put it on his head.  
He looked around and saw a small blue boat tied to the wall.  
"Can I go in?" he asked the man who stood nearby.  
The man smiled and said, "Yes, but only if you follow the path."  
The boy followed the path and saw a green beacon shining in the distance.  
He ran toward it, laughing, because it looked like a star.  
He stopped and saw a sign that said, "Come home, friend."  
He walked back to the house and saw his mother waiting with a cup of tea.  
She said, "You were lost, but you found your way."  
He looked at the beacon again and smiled.  
He knew he would always find his way back, even in the dark.  
The next morning, he brought a small box with a new toy to his friend.  
They sat together and watched the sun rise, happy and full of hope.

The sea was calm that morning.  
She sat on the porch and watched the waves go by.  
A small boat floated in the harbor, quiet and still.  
The wind was not loud, just a soft breath.  
She opened her book and read about fish in the sea.  
One red bird landed on the wall and looked at her.  
She smiled and said, "Good morning, little one."  
The bird jumped and flew into the garden.  
She felt calm, like the world had slowed down.  
She saw a cat sleeping on the floor.  
The cat looked up and gave her a slow blink.  
She thought, "This is a quiet day."  
She wanted to stay here, with the sea and the sun.  
She knew she would come back again.

The sun came out in the morning and made the garden feel warm.  
She sat on a chair and looked at the meadow beyond the wall.  
A little girl ran into the meadow and stopped to watch a red bird.  
She smiled and said, “That bird is so quiet.”  
The meadow had soft grass and flowers that moved in the wind.  
She found a blue box under a tree and opened it.  
Inside, there was a small key and a note that said, “Find your friend.”  
She looked around and saw a dog sleeping near a flower.  
She called the dog and it jumped up, wagging its tail.  
The meadow was full of quiet sounds and soft laughter.  
She thought, “Maybe the key opens a secret place.”  
She walked slowly and found a small red hat on the floor.  
She picked it up and felt it was warm and soft.  
She knew this was the place where her friend lived.  
She sat down and said, “I will come back tomorrow.”

The boy stood in the garden and looked up at the sky.  
He saw a bright light moving fast across the sky.  
"Is that a star?" he asked his friend.  
She said, "No, it's a comet."  
He didn't know what a comet was, but it looked like a long, thin piece of ice.  
The comet moved slowly, leaving a trail of light behind.  
He wanted to follow it, so he ran to the park.  
There, he saw another comet, this one glowing red and spinning.  
He felt excited and said, "I love comets!"  
He sat on the grass and watched them go away.  
The next morning, he found a book about space and read about comets.  
He showed it to his dog, who wagged his tail and barked.  
He thought, "Comets are fun and quiet, like a story from the sky."  
He promised to look for comets every night.  
He even drew a picture of a comet with a tail and a name.

The wind blew hard in the mountain town.  
A girl sat by the window, watching the cold sky.  
She saw a big white shape far away on the hill.  
It looked like a wall, but it moved slowly.  
She asked her friend if it was a new kind of cloud.  
Her friend said, "No, it's a glacier."  
She felt cold, but also happy to see something so big.  
The next morning, she went to the park to look again.  
She saw the glacier had grown a little, like a slow-moving cat.  
She sat on a chair and watched it for hours.  
It looked quiet, but it made a soft sound when the wind passed.  
She thought, "This is not just ice — it is a world of cold."  
Later, she found a small red book near the door.  
The book said, "Glaciers are old, but they remember the sun."  
She smiled and knew she would go back again.

The boy stood in the garden and looked at the big stone on the wall.  
He had found it there last week and thought it looked like a cat.  
He wanted to make something new with it, so he picked up a hammer and started to break it.  
But the stone was too strong and he couldn’t break it.  
Then he saw a man in a red hat walking by and asked, “Can you help me?”  
The man said, “I don’t know how to fix stone, but I know a sculptor.”  
The boy felt happy and ran to the park to find the sculptor.  
The sculptor sat on a chair and said, “I can turn stone into a bird.”  
The boy watched and saw how the stone became a small, flying bird.  
He laughed and said, “That’s the best thing I’ve ever seen!”  
The sculptor smiled and said, “I make things from stone, like birds, trees, and houses.”  
The boy wanted to try again and asked, “Can you make a new one?”  
The sculptor said, “Yes, but only if you bring me a big stone.”  
The boy ran home and found a stone under the tree.  
He brought it to the sculptor and said, “This one is proud.”  
The sculptor laughed and said, “That’s the best word I’ve ever heard.”

The sun came out in the morning and warmed the ground.  
We walked through the garden and saw a big red apple on a tree.  
A little girl stood there and said, "Can I pick one?"  
She reached for the apple and smiled.  
The wind blew and made the leaves move.  
Behind the house, there was a quiet orchard with many trees.  
We went there and found a box full of apples.  
She picked one and ate it, and then she laughed.  
The warm light made her feel happy.  
She said, "This is the best day ever."  
We sat on a chair and watched the birds fly.  
The orchard was full of birds and warm air.  
She told a story about a cat that jumped into a box.  
We all laughed and said, "That was funny!"  
The day ended with a sweet smell of apples.

The girl sat by the window in her small house and watched the rain fall outside.  
She had a red hat and a book about animals.  
In the garden, a little cat jumped over a fence and ran into the yard.  
She called out, “Come here, little one!” but the cat just looked away.  
Then she saw a small box under the table with a blue light inside.  
She opened it and found a lantern inside, glowing softly.  
She held it close and felt warm, like the sun had come through the clouds.  
That night, she placed the lantern on her nightstand and smiled, knowing it would keep her safe.  
She told her friend, “I found a lantern,” and they both laughed.  
The next morning, the lantern was still lit, even though no one had turned it on.  
She asked, “How does it work?” and her friend said, “It just knows when you need light.”  
She carried it to the garden and placed it near the tree.  
Later, a bird landed on the roof and looked at it with a funny face.  
She said, “It’s not broken,” and the lantern stayed bright.  
She sat again, and the light made the room feel cozy and full of peace.

The boy found a red box in the garden.  
He opened it and saw a small blue compass inside.  
It pointed straight to the old tree in the park.  
He followed the direction and found a hidden door.  
The door was locked, but he had a key from his mother.  
He used the key and went inside.  
The room was full of books and a cat sat on the chair.  
He asked the cat, "Do you know the way out?"  
The cat blinked and said, "Follow the compass, it will guide you."  
He looked around and saw a map drawn on the wall.  
The map showed a path to the river.  
He walked fast and saw a bird flying high.  
The bird dropped a piece of cheese and he picked it up.  
He smiled and felt happy.  
He knew the compass would always help him find his way.

The boy walked into the park and saw a big red rock on the edge.  
He picked it up and felt it was warm and smooth.  
Then he saw a small blue bird sitting on a tree branch.  
The bird looked at him and made a funny sound.  
He laughed and ran back to his house.  
His mother asked, "What did you find?"  
He said, "I found a canyon!"  
She looked surprised and said, "There’s no canyon here!"  
He showed her the rock and said, "It’s not a rock — it’s a canyon!"  
She looked again and saw the rock was wide and deep.  
They sat on the floor and watched the sun go down.  
The boy smiled and said, "I think the canyon is inside the rock."  
That night, he dreamed of a canyon with a river and a cat.  
He woke up and said, "I saw a canyon in my dream!"  
His friend said, "You must have been very happy."  
He said, "Yes, because I feel like I found a canyon in my heart."

She lived in a small house on a quiet street.  
The garden had flowers and a red door.  
One morning, she saw a little bird sitting on the roof.  
She called the bird and it jumped down to her hand.  
She gave it a piece of cheese and it smiled.  
Later, she found a broken box in the garden.  
Inside, she saw a key and a note that said "cottage".  
She thought, "I need to go to a cottage."  
She walked to the edge of the park and saw a cottage with a green roof.  
She knocked on the door and a woman opened it.  
The woman said, "This is my cottage. I live here."  
She asked, "Can I stay for a day?"  
The woman said yes and gave her a key.  
She felt happy and warm, like home.  
She sat on a chair and read a book by the window.

The sun shone bright in the morning, and the children ran through the park.  
A big red bird flew over the hill and landed on a tree.  
She saw a small blue box near the wall and opened it.  
Inside, there was a key and a note that said, "Find the waterfall."  
They all looked around, but no waterfall was visible.  
One boy said, "Maybe it's behind the garden."  
They walked through the garden and found a path that went down the hill.  
At the bottom, the water fell fast and made a loud sound — a real waterfall!  
They laughed and sat on the floor, drinking warm tea.  
The waterfall was not far from their house, and they knew they would go back again.  
They made a new friend and told a story about the key.  
The next day, they came with a dog and a book.  
They found the same key and the same note, and the same waterfall.  
They all felt happy and knew they would never stop playing.

The boy stood in the garden and looked up at the sky.  
He saw a bright red ball floating in the blue sky.  
"Is that a star?" he asked his friend.  
She said, "No, it's just a balloon."  
But he kept looking, and then he saw a small green shape near the tree.  
He ran to the edge of the park and found a box with a red hat.  
Inside, he found a key and a note that said, "Look through the window."  
He opened the window and saw a big telescope on the table.  
He held it and looked through it at the stars.  
The stars looked like fish swimming in a dark sea.  
He smiled and said, "This is the best day ever."  
He sat down and read the note again.  
"Look through the window," it said.  
He did it again and saw a new star.  
He showed it to his friend, and she laughed.  
They both knew that the telescope had a secret.

The cat sat on the windowsill and watched the garden all morning.  
She saw a small mouse run past the flower bed and jump into the grass.  
The dog barked loudly and ran after it, but the mouse was gone.  
No one saw the mouse again, and the family started to feel quiet.  
Then, a man in a red hat walked into the garden and stood still.  
He looked around with a very **vigilant** eye and didn’t move.  
He saw a red box under the tree and picked it up slowly.  
The next day, the children found the box open and inside was a note that said, “Stay safe.”  
They all felt calm again, and the garden stayed peaceful.  
The man never came back, but the children still watch the garden with **vigilant** eyes.  
They say the mouse still hides in the garden at night, and they stay **vigilant** to hear a small sound.  
One day, a bird flew in and dropped a piece of cheese — the children laughed and ran to catch it.  
They didn’t see the mouse, but they stayed **vigilant** and looked at every corner.  
Now, every morning, they sit on the porch and watch the garden, just like the cat.

The cat sat on the windowsill and looked at the bird outside.  
It was a blue bird with a red hat.  
The bird said, "I see a mouse!" and jumped into the garden.  
The cat didn't move. It was very hungry.  
He looked at the cheese on the table and felt his stomach grow.  
He wanted to eat it fast.  
Then he saw a small red box under the chair.  
He opened it and found a new kind of food.  
He ate it and felt ravenous.  
He ran to the park and found a friend.  
They both laughed and ate more food.  
The friend said, "You are ravenous!"  
He smiled and said, "Yes, I am!"  
They sat on the grass and watched the sun go down.  
The cat said, "I love this food!"  
He felt happy and full.  
He went home and slept with a full belly.

The sky was deep blue and soft, like a blanket over the town.  
She sat by the window and watched the sun go down, glowing warm and golden.  
A little bird flew in and landed on the table, its feathers bright and shiny.  
She didn’t know why, but she felt a strange glow in her chest.  
It wasn’t fire or light — it was something quiet, like a whisper from the wind.  
Then she saw a box in the corner with a red lid, and it was open.  
Inside, a small fish swam slowly, its body glowing with a soft, luminous light.  
She reached out and touched it — the light spread through her hand, warm and happy.  
She smiled and said, “This is not a dream.”  
The fish jumped up and swam to the wall, where it stayed, glowing gently.  
She sat again, and the glow stayed with her, like a friend.  
She told her dog, “I saw something beautiful today.”  
The dog looked at her and gave a quiet bark, like he knew.  
She didn’t ask more — she just knew it was true.  
And every night, when she closed her eyes, she saw the fish, glowing softly in the dark.

The cat sat in the quiet garden all night.  
She didn’t move, even when the wind blew through the trees.  
A small mouse ran past, but she stayed still.  
No one saw her, not even the red bird on the fence.  
She felt warm in her fur, like the sun had come back.  
The world was silent, and she liked it that way.  
Then she heard a soft *click* — a tiny box had opened.  
Inside was a piece of cheese, and she didn’t know why.  
She looked at it and smiled, because it was so quiet.  
She didn’t want to move, not even to eat.  
The next morning, the garden was full of birds.  
But she still stayed in the corner, watching the sky.  
She knew she was not like the others — she was quiet.  
She loved the night, and the stillness, and the peace.

The sun came out slowly over the garden.  
She sat on the chair by the window and watched the birds fly.  
The wind was quiet and the world felt calm.  
A small red bird landed on the table and looked at her.  
She smiled and said, “Hello.”  
The bird didn’t move, just stayed still.  
She thought, “This is peaceful.”  
She wanted to stay like this forever.  
The next morning, she found a blue box under the tree.  
Inside was a key and a note that said, “Tranquil.”  
She opened it and saw a picture of the same bird.  
She felt happy and warm.  
She sat again, this time with her hat on.  
The bird came back and sat on her lap.  
She knew this was a peaceful day.  
She would come back every morning to feel tranquil.

The boy sat on the porch and watched the rain fall slowly.  
He saw a red bird hop from branch to branch, moving in a slow way.  
That made him think — maybe the rain was making the world move slow.  
He opened his book and read about a river that meandered through the forest.  
The river looked like it was walking in a long, winding path.  
He smiled and said, “That’s how the wind moves too.”  
His friend came over and asked, “Do you see how the grass bends slowly?”  
She pointed to a field where the wind made the blades move in a slow wave.  
They both knew that slow things could be beautiful.  
The boy closed his book and said, “I want to walk with the wind.”  
He stood up and walked down the street, feeling the slow rhythm of the day.  
The sun came out, warm and quiet, and the world felt still.  
He sat again, watching a cat walk slowly past the house.  
He thought, “Even cats move slow when they’re happy.”  
The day ended with a quiet smile and a slow breath.

The cat sat on the windowsill and watched the garden.  
A small mouse ran past and hid behind the old red door.  
He looked around and saw a furtive shadow move in the grass.  
The mouse stopped and listened—something was wrong.  
It didn’t look like a bird, or a dog, or even a cat.  
It just moved too fast, too quiet, and too close.  
The mouse felt cold, like the wind had touched his fur.  
He didn’t know what it was, but he knew it wasn’t friendly.  
Then the shadow jumped into the garden and vanished.  
The mouse ran to the door and saw a furtive box under the table.  
Inside, a piece of cheese was wrapped in a blue cloth.  
He didn’t want to eat it, but he couldn’t stop looking.  
The box had a name: “Milo.”  
He didn’t know who made it, but he knew it was not for him.  
Later that night, the mouse found Milo again—this time, in the kitchen.

It was a quiet afternoon in the garden when the wind started to howl.  
A black cat sat on the wall, watching the sky.  
She saw a red box near the door and felt something strange.  
The box had a lock, and the key was missing.  
No one had seen it before, but the air felt heavy.  
Everyone in the house said, “It’s just the wind.”  
But the cat didn’t move when the storm came.  
The storm was loud, and the trees bent low.  
Then, a small bird flew in and dropped a note.  
The note said: “Look inside. It’s not a box.”  
Inside, the box was full of old books, all with the same name.  
One book had a word that made her stop: *ominous*.  
She opened it and saw a picture of a man with a hat.  
He looked at her and smiled, but the sky turned dark.  
She knew then that the house had a secret.

The boy sat at the kitchen table and looked at the empty bowl.  
He felt hungry and wanted something to eat.  
His mother said, "Let’s go to the garden."  
In the garden, he saw a big red tomato on the wall.  
He picked it up and took a bite.  
It was sweet and made him smile.  
Then he saw a small mouse running through the grass.  
The mouse looked at him and gave a quick jump.  
He felt very hungry again and wanted more food.  
He ran to the kitchen and asked for a new sandwich.  
His mother said, "You have a voracious appetite."  
He didn’t know what that meant, but he liked the sound of it.  
Later that night, he sat on the floor and thought about the mouse.  
He said, "I want to feed the mouse."  
The next morning, he found a box with a red hat inside.  
He opened it and saw a note: "You are very hungry, but also very kind."  
He smiled and felt happy.

The girl found a big red rock in the garden.  
She picked it up and looked at it closely.  
It felt cold and smooth, like a piece of old stone.  
She asked her dog if it was a fossil.  
The dog barked and ran away, but she stayed.  
She sat on the floor and watched the sun go down.  
She thought, "Maybe it's a fossil from long ago."  
She dug around the base of the tree and found a small bone.  
She held it in her hand and smiled.  
She knew now that the red rock was not a fossil.  
She looked at the bone and said, "That's not a fossil either."  
She ran to the window and saw a bird flying past.  
She laughed and said, "I didn’t know fossils were so small!"  
She went back to the garden and found another red rock.  
She picked it up and said, "This one feels warm, not cold."  
She knew the truth: not all rocks are fossils.

The girl found a small red ball in the garden.  
She picked it up and smiled.  
It was soft and warm in her hand.  
She asked her dog if it wanted to play.  
The dog jumped and ran after it.  
She saw a hedgehog sleeping under the wall.  
It had a tiny blue hat and looked very quiet.  
She sat down and told the story of how the ball came from the tree.  
The hedgehog opened one eye and looked at her.  
She gave it a piece of cheese and it ate slowly.  
The hedgehog stood up and walked to the door.  
She followed it and saw it go into the garden again.  
They played together every morning.  
The hedgehog never ran away.  
She felt happy and knew it was a good friend.

The girl found a small red box under the garden chair.  
Inside, the box had a key that looked like a fish.  
She opened it and saw a blue hat with a tiny gold star.  
She picked it up and felt it warm in her hand.  
That night, the hat began to *shimmer* softly.  
She sat by the window and watched the stars go *shimmer* above the tree.  
The wind came in and made the hat *shimmer* like a wave of light.  
She smiled and said, “This is a good friend.”  
She gave the hat to her dog, who sat still and let it *shimmer* on his head.  
The next morning, the hat was gone — but the floor still *shimmered* where it had been.  
She looked at the wall and saw a small mark that *shimmered* like a secret.  
She asked her cat, “Did you see the hat?”  
The cat blinked and said, “Yes, it *shimmered* when I walked past.”  
She ran to the park and found a new red box — it *shimmered* with a quiet laugh.  
She knew then that magic was not far, and the world was full of quiet *shimmer*.

The path wound slowly through the forest, bending around big trees.  
He followed the path, thinking it would lead to the lake.  
It was quiet, and the wind moved like a snake.  
He saw a red bird sitting on a branch, watching the path.  
The path looked serpentine, twisting in circles.  
He stopped and looked at the sky, which was blue and warm.  
A small mouse ran past, hiding behind a stone.  
He smiled, because the path felt like a long, slow dance.  
The next day, he walked the same path again, and it still looked serpentine.  
He found a key under a tree, and it felt cold in his hand.  
He didn’t know where it led, but he wanted to follow it.  
The path bent again, going down a hill and into the garden.  
He sat on a chair and read a book about animals.  
He saw a cat jump over a fence, and the path looked like a snake again.  
He knew he would keep walking, because the path was full of quiet stories.

The girl sat on the porch, watching the rain fall.  
She had no hat, no coat, and no one to play with.  
The garden was full of weeds, and the flowers had gone quiet.  
She looked at the red door and thought, *I don’t want to go inside.*  
A small mouse ran past, and she felt sad again.  
She opened the window and saw a blue bird flying away.  
She wanted to call her friend, but the phone was broken.  
She sat there, forlorn, with a book in her lap, waiting for the sun to come back.  
She didn’t move, and the world felt cold and still.  
She thought of the time they played in the park, and how happy they had been.  
Now, she only saw the empty chair and the silence.  
She closed her eyes and felt the cold floor beneath her.  
She didn’t know if she would ever feel warm again.  
She stayed there, forlorn, until the morning light came.

The wind howled outside the window.  
It was a cold morning in the town of Maplewood.  
Snow fell all day, and the sky turned white.  
The garden looked quiet, with no birds or children playing.  
A big red hat sat on the table, forgotten.  
She opened the door and saw a small dog hiding under the chair.  
The dog looked sad and hungry.  
Then, a loud noise came from the back of the house.  

A blizzard had started.  
The whole street was covered in snow.  
No one could go out to the park.  
The children stayed inside, reading books.  
One boy said, “I want to go outside and play.”  
But the wind was too strong.  
He looked at the window and saw a red hat again.  
He smiled and said, “I’ll stay here and watch the snow.”  

The blizzard lasted all night.  
The next morning, the snow melted.  
The garden looked green again.  
The dog ran outside and barked.  
Everyone laughed and said, “It was a funny day.”

The girl walked into the old house and saw a dark room with no light.  
She asked the cat if it knew what was behind the wall.  
The cat meowed and jumped into a small box.  
She opened the box and found a red key inside.  
She felt cold and sat on the floor, afraid of what it might open.  
She looked at the key and thought, "This is not a key for a door."  
Then she saw a small stone on the floor, and it looked like a name.  
She picked it up and said, "This is not a stone. It's a clue."  
She went to the back of the house and found a cellar under the garden.  
She opened the cellar door and saw a blue book with a strange word: *und*.  
She read it and laughed, because it looked like a word for "stop."  
She closed the book and felt warm, like the sun had come in.  
She sat on the chair and said, "I think I found the secret."  
She looked at the key again and knew it was meant for the cellar.  
She smiled and said, "I have the key. I have the clue. I have the story."

The girl sat on the floor and looked at the box of toys.  
She asked her dog, "Can you solve this?"  
The dog barked and ran to the garden.  
A red bird flew in and said, "I have a riddle for you."  
She smiled and said, "Tell me!"  
The bird said, "What has keys but can't open locks?"  
She thought for a moment and said, "A piano!"  
The bird laughed and said, "Yes! That’s the answer!"  
They all sat together and told a new story.  
The next day, she found a riddle in her book.  
She read it and said, "I can’t solve this one."  
Her friend came and said, "Try again!"  
They sat on the chair and looked at the sky.  
The riddle was about a cat and a mouse.  
She said, "I know the answer now!"  
They all laughed and went to the park.

The boat sat still in the calm sea.  
It didn’t move, even when the wind blew hard.  
The man sat on the deck and watched the waves.  
He felt safe, like a bird that had found its home.  
He looked at the red hat on the floor and smiled.  
A small fish jumped out of the water and swam away.  
He called his friend, who came with a box of cheese.  
They sat together and told a story about a storm.  
The storm had come fast, but the boat stayed steady.  
He held the hat and said, “This is my anchor.”  
The cat jumped on the chair and looked at the sky.  
They all felt calm again, like the sea had stopped moving.  
He knew the boat would never fall apart.  
They stayed there, happy and quiet, for the whole morning.

The sun rose bright over the red roof of the old house.  
A cat sat on the windowsill, watching the birds fly in.  
She saw a small mouse run into the garden.  
The mouse hid under a big rock, then jumped out again.  
It was a funny sight, and everyone laughed.  
She called her friend to come and see.  
They found a red box with a key inside.  
The key was cold, but it opened the door to the garden.  
Inside, the grass was green and the flowers were bright.  
She felt happy, and the wind blew softly.  
Then, a loud noise came from the tree.  
A fire started in the corner of the garden.  
The flames spread fast, and the whole place turned red.  
She ran to the door and shouted, “Help!”  
But the fire was too big to stop.  
She sat down and thought, “This is not good.”  
Then she saw a small blue bird flying away.  
She smiled and said, “Maybe tomorrow will be better.”

She found a small pebble by the garden door.  
It was round and smooth, like a little stone from the river.  
She picked it up and held it in her hand.  
The pebble felt cold, but she liked it.  
She put it in her pocket and walked to the park.  
A dog ran past and barked, but she didn’t stop.  
She sat on the bench and looked at the sky.  
The pebble was quiet, and she felt happy.  
She gave it to her friend, who said, “It’s a nice pebble.”  
They both smiled and sat together.  
She found another pebble near the tree.  
It was red and looked like a tiny berry.  
She put it in her pocket too.  
She told her mom about the pebble, and she said, “That’s a good one.”  
She kept the pebbles in her pocket and felt warm.  
She knew she would find more pebbles every day.

The boy sat in the garden and watched the birds fly over the green roof.  
He saw a red box on the floor and picked it up.  
Inside, there was a small key and a note that said *“Find the gorge.”*  
He didn’t know what a gorge was, but he thought it might be a deep hole.  
He ran through the garden and found a big crack in the wall.  
It opened into a dark cave with cold air and a strange smell.  
He stood still and listened — the cave was full of quiet sounds.  
Then he saw a small blue fish swimming in the water below.  
He laughed and said, “This is not a gorge — it’s a secret room!”  
He sat down and read the note again.  
The key fit the lock on the door, and he opened it.  
Inside, there was a book with a picture of a cat and a boy.  
He smiled and said, “I found the gorge — and it was a surprise.”  
He looked around and saw a new red hat on the table.  
He knew he had to go home, but he wanted to come back again.

The old farm had a big red barn at the back.  
Inside, a cat sat on a chair and watched the birds fly.  
A small mouse ran into the barn and hid under a box.  
The mouse looked at a blue hat and thought, "That’s my favorite color."  
He saw a hole in the wall and jumped in, not knowing what was inside.  
There, a dog lay on the floor, eating cheese and sleeping.  
The dog looked up and said, "You’re not supposed to be here!"  
The mouse felt scared and ran out, but then saw a new sign on the door: "Welcome to the barn."  
He smiled and went back in, because it felt warm and safe.  
The next morning, a child came to the farm and asked, "Can I play in the barn?"  
She found a toy horse and sat on the floor, laughing.  
The dog barked and ran to the door, but stopped when he saw the mouse again.  
They played together, and the barn felt like home.  
No one ever said it was bad — it was just quiet and full of happy sounds.

The sun came out in the morning and painted the sky bright blue.  
She walked through the garden and saw a big red flower growing near the wall.  
A little girl sat on a chair and looked at the flower with a happy smile.  
She asked the wind, “Do you know what this flower is for?”  
The wind whispered, “It helps the bees find the meadow.”  
She ran to the meadow and saw bees buzzing around a yellow flower.  
A small bird landed on a branch and sang a soft song.  
She felt warm and happy, like the sun had touched her heart.  
She looked up and saw a tall light shining in the sky — a beacon.  
She didn’t know why, but she thought it was a sign that good things would come.  
She sat down and watched the light glow, like a friend from the sky.  
The next day, she found a new red flower in the garden, and the beacon shone again.  
She smiled and said, “I know the meadow is full of hope.”  
She knew the light would stay, and the garden would grow, one day at a time.

The boy found a red box in the garden.  
He opened it and saw a small blue lantern inside.  
It was cold, but the light came on when he turned it.  
He took it to the cellar and put it on the table.  
The cellar was dark and quiet, with only one old chair.  
He sat down and waited for something to happen.  
Then the lantern began to glow, and the walls looked warm.  
He saw a small mouse run past the door.  
He laughed and said, "This is not a normal house!"  
The next morning, he found the lantern on the floor again.  
He picked it up and carried it to the window.  
The light showed a path through the garden.  
He followed it and found a new book under the tree.  
He opened it and read a story about a boy with a lantern.  
He smiled and knew he would go back again.

The boat sat in the harbor, waiting for the tide to rise.  
Every morning, the old man went to the harbor to check the weather.  
He had a red hat and a small box with a key inside.  
One day, he saw a blue bird flying over the sea.  
He asked the bird, "Do you know where the lost boat is?"  
The bird said, "It’s far out, past the green island."  
He took the key and opened the box.  
Inside was a map with a red mark — a new voyage.  
He felt calm and began to walk toward the sea.  
The wind was strong, but he didn’t stop.  
He saw a small boat floating in the water — it was his own.  
He smiled and said, "This is the start of a new voyage."  
He sat on the floor and read the map again.  
The sun came out, and the sea looked warm.  
He knew he had to go — the voyage was calling him.

The boy walked into the garden and saw a big red apple on the tree.  
He felt hungry and wanted to eat it.  
He looked behind the wall and saw a small blue bird.  
The bird was not singing — it was just sitting still.  
He asked the bird, “Do you know where the apple is?”  
The bird said, “No, but I can help you find it.”  
Then he saw a box under the tree with a key inside.  
He opened it and found a fossil of a small cat.  
He was ravenous and wanted to eat the fossil.  
He laughed and said, “This is not food!”  
The fossil was not made of meat — it was made of stone.  
He sat down and thought, “Maybe I should go to the orchard.”  
He walked to the orchard and saw many trees with apples.  
He picked one and ate it — it was sweet and warm.  
He smiled and said, “I love the orchard.”

tuned_rows.story: The night was very cold and the rain came down on the old house. A small bird sat in the window and looked out at the dark street. The bird was a beacon. She was bright and red and she called to all the lost birds in the rain. They see her and they come to her, like they find a warm home. A cat by the door looked up at the beacon bird and then looked up at the window too. The cat is hungry, and he wants to get into the house. A man in the garden sees the cat and the bird and he starts to laugh. He is kind, and he opens the door for the cat. The cat jumps in, and the man gives him some food and a funny hat. Now the cat is happy and he sits on the chair by the wall. The bird in the window is warm and she does not feel sad any more. She is a beacon, and her light is good to all who need to come close. The man and the cat read a book by the window in the morning sun. They find a small box of old cheese on the table, and they eat and laugh. The bird on the wall looks down and she too smiles. There is no bad day when a little light can help find a new day.

The old house on the green street has a small harbor. Water and a big wall, like a quiet garden by the sea. A cat and a dog live there, and they love the harbor. The water is calm, and the sun is warm. The cat sit on the wall and look at the water. The dog run to the water and then stop, very quiet. He is hungry, but he find a small fish in a box by the door. The dog eat the fish and feel happy again. The cat see him eat and smile. They sit in the harbor and do not run or jump. The harbor is a good place to be when the rain come in. The dog ask the cat to go out into the water, but the cat say no. They sit close, and the dog sleep on the floor. In the morning, a bird come to the old tree and tell a new story. The two friends hear the bird and laugh a little. They open the door, and the warm sun go in with the bird. Now they work, and they read a book, and they feel like home. The harbor is here, and so is good.

The sun is warm and the day is good. A small girl walks to the meadow. The meadow is big and green and full of flowers. She sits down on the soft grass and closes her eyes. The meadow is quiet and kind to her. A blue bird sits in the tree close to her. She opens her eyes and smiles at the bird. The bird looks at her too, and she feels happy. She wants to find a new place to play, so she stands up. She walks to the old tree and sees a small cat hide in the box. The cat is funny and slow, and she laughs. She goes back to the meadow and sits in the warm sun. She is not sad now. She is not hungry now. She has some food and a book, and she reads a story out loud. The dog comes to the door of the house and calls to her. She runs to him and they play in the garden. Then they go to the park, and the meadow is there too, at the top of the hill. She and the dog sit in the meadow at night and look at the stars. It is a very good day, and the meadow is a good friend.

The boy look out the window and see a big, bright light in the sky. He ask his friend, "Do you know that?". His friend say, "No, I do not know that". The boy tell him a story. A story of a comet. A comet is a cold, funny, old star that come down from far, far away and make the sky so bright. The boy say, "A comet is like a big, slow, red fish that fly up into the sky". His friend laugh and laugh. They sit in the room and read a book about comets. The book say a comet come to the garden one night and the sun go away. The cat run out and the dog run out too, and they all hide in the box. The boy open the door and the comet go away. Now the sun come back and the rain stop. The boy feel happy and warm. He give his friend some cheese and they sit on the chair and play. The cat and the dog come in too, and they all laugh and sleep here. The boy think, a comet is a very good friend when you need a new story.

The sun is out, but the day is very cold.

A big glacier is close to the mountain. It is a great, slow wall of ice.

The girl in the red hat walks near the glacier and looks up at it.

She wants to know more, so she asks her friend for a book.

In the book, she reads that a glacier is made of old ice that is very, very cold.

The dog runs to the door, but the cold wind comes in too.

The cat sits on the chair and does not want to go out.

The girl and her friend find some food and eat it in the warm room.

Then they go out again and look at the glacier. It is blue and white and big.

A small bird comes down to the garden, but the cold rain is here too.

The girl and her friend laugh and say the cold is funny.

They find a box near the old tree and open it. There is cheese and a key in it!

The dog is happy now, and the cat comes to the window to see them.

They all sit and eat, and the girl tells a new story to her friend.

The two friends sleep in the warm house, and they do not forget this cold day.

I like to make big, good statues out of old stone. I work in my garden, and I do not stop. One day, a friend comes to the door and asks to see my new work. She walk close to the wall and look up at the statue. It is a cat, and it is very funny. She laugh out loud, and I smile too. I tell her the cat is named Blue, and she say she love the name. Then a dog run into the garden and jump on the old stone. No, not run. Jump, quick and loud, like a small bird. I call him, but he stop and look at me with one big eye. I ask him to sit, and he sit on the floor of my garden. Now I feel happy, and I feel proud. A proud man stand in his garden and make good statues. I think a proud man do not hide his work, and I do not hide it too. My friend come again in the morning, and she find a new cat statue in the rain. She say the cold water make the old stone look very blue, and she laugh again. I feel proud when she laugh, and I feel proud when she say yes, come and help me make more. I have two old boxes, and I have some old stone, and I love to work with them. A proud sculptor tell his story to all the people on the street.

The sun is warm and the day is good.

There is a big garden with old trees, and in that garden there is an orchard. A place where all the trees live and make sweet apples.

A girl walks to the orchard and looks up at all the green trees.

She sees a small bird in the orchard and she is very happy.

The warm sun is good for the orchard, and the trees need it to grow.

The girl and the bird sit close to one tree in the warm orchard.

She is hungry, so she goes to her house to find some food.

Her friend is there too, and they open a big box of cheese and fish.

They eat and laugh, and the warm sun makes the room feel kind and good.

The girl asks her friend to read her a story, and she finds an old book on the table.

They sit in the warm room, and the quiet is very good there.

Then the rain starts to come down, and they run out to the orchard.

The rain is cold, but the sun is warm again very quick.

They stand in the orchard and look at the water on the old trees.

The girl is happy now, and she knows the orchard is a good place to be.

The old woman lived in a small house by the garden. She had a big room with a window, and in that room she kept a lantern. The lantern was old, but it gave a warm light when the night came. At night, the woman would open the door and walk out with the lantern to look for food in the garden. She loved to carry the lantern, and her cat and her dog would run to her hand when she walked out with it. One cold night, the lantern did not open. No light, no help, no good light. The woman stood there and felt sad, for the lantern was her friend. Then she found a key in a box by the door, and she used the key to open the lantern. Now the room was warm and the garden was good and close. She sat in her chair, and the cat and the dog came to sit with her. The little bird in the tree looked in at the window, and the woman smiled. She told a funny story to the cat, and they all start to laugh. The dog wanted to play, so the woman said, "Yes, dog, come here!" They ran and jumped in the room, and the cat jumped too. The woman said, "This is a very happy home." The old lantern stood on the table, warm and quiet, and they all sleep, and the night is not bad now.

Ben the boy love to find new places to play.

One day he walk out of his house and into the park, but then he feel very lost.

He need a way to know the way home, so he ask his friend for a compass.

A compass is a small old book with a funny blue hand in it that point the way.

Ben open his box and find a new compass, and the blue hand in it point to the sun.

He look at the compass and smile, and he start to walk in that way.

The rain come down, and a cat run out of a house, but Ben not stop.

He hear a bird in the tree, and he laugh, for the bird look very funny.

Then he see a big dog close to the wall, and the dog look too funny.

Ben and the dog sit down and eat some food by the door of an old house.

The dog is hungry too, so Ben give him some cheese, and they feel warm and happy.

At night, Ben look at his compass one more time, and the blue hand point to his home.

He jump up, run quick, and get to the door of his house very fast.

His friend call out to him and say, "Come in! You need to sleep!"

Ben smile and say, "I love my compass. I trust it all day and all night."

The boy and his dog walk to the park one morning.

They find a big canyon there. So wide and so deep it goes all the way down to the water.

The dog runs to the edge of the canyon and looks in.

The boy and the dog stand very close to the wall of the canyon, and the boy feels a funny, happy kind of awe.

He is so awed that he can not say a word.

His friend comes to the park and sees the canyon too.

She is awed, and she stands very slow and very quiet at the edge of the canyon.

They sit on a old chair and read a book and eat some food.

The dog is hungry and wants to go home, but the boy is too awed to go.

Then they hear a loud bird in the green tree up in the canyon.

The bird is red and blue and it calls out in a funny way.

The boy and his friend laugh, and the dog jumps up and runs to catch a small mouse.

They all feel good again, and the boy thinks: this canyon is a very good place to live.

I love my small house on the green street. It is a cottage, a very old but very warm little home. The cottage has a red door and a blue roof. I walk in and I feel happy, like home. A cat and a dog live in the cottage too. The dog is big and the cat is small, but they do not feel bad or sad here. One morning, a new friend knock at the door. She is a kind woman with a funny hat. We sit in the cottage and read a book and eat some cheese and fish. The cottage has a small garden out the window, and we see a bird in the tree. Then the rain start to come, so we close the door and laugh. The cat find a mouse in the old box on the floor, and we all laugh again. I tell her a story, and she ask me to tell it one more day. The cottage is good, and so is this friend. We work and play and sleep in the warm room, and I know I want to come to this little home again and again.

The waterfall is very big. It falls down from a high hill and makes a loud, funny sound. I go to the park to see the waterfall one morning. The water is cold and blue and it runs all the way to the green garden. A small cat sits close to the wall and looks up at the waterfall. The cat is very happy there. A dog runs to the waterfall too, and the water is so big that the dog has to stop and laugh. A bird in the tree hears the dog and jumps into the water. The bird is not sad. It is happy, and the water is good for her. We all want to get some water to drink, but the waterfall is too fast to catch. A friend and I find a big old box by the door of the house, and we open it to find a funny red hat and some old food. The cold water make the room feel good and warm. I tell a new story to my friend: a cat, a dog, and a bird at the waterfall. They all know the word now. We sit by the window and read the story again and again. The waterfall is home to all of us.

I have a small telescope. It is old and blue, but I love it.

Every morning, I sit at the window and look through the telescope at the sun.

I see the tree in the garden, and I see the bird in the tree, and I see the cat on the wall.

One day, I ask my friend to come and look with me through the telescope.

We find a funny little mouse near the old box, and we laugh out loud.

I want to know more, so I open a book and read a story about a telescope.

The story tells of a girl who has a telescope and she sees a new star every night.

I feel happy when I look through the telescope at night, and I do not feel sad.

My dog is hungry, but he is slow, and the rain is cold, so I stop him.

We sit close to the window, and I tell him his name, and he is happy too.

Then I see the moon up there, big and warm, and I feel like a kind friend.

I think: this small telescope and this old chair and this quiet room make a good home for wondering.

I look out again and again, and I find that wondering is the best kind of work.

The old cat in the green house is very vigilant. She does not sleep, she does not play, she stands up and looks out the window all night.

Her two small eyes do not close, and she is vigilant every morning, every night, every day.

One day, a bird comes to the window and she is vigilant again, and she jumps up to catch it.

But the bird is funny and it flies past, and the cat is sad.

Then a dog runs into the garden with a big red hat and some food, and the vigilant cat runs out too.

The dog is happy and he asks the cat to come and eat, but the cat says no.

"You need to be vigilant," the cat says, "or a mouse will get your food."

The dog is kind and he gives the cat some cheese, and they sit by the door and read a old book.

In the quiet room, the vigilant cat and the hungry dog find a new story to tell and laugh at.

Biscuit the dog is very hungry. He is ravenous, a funny kind of hungry that makes him want to eat all day and all night. Biscuit is ravenous for food, for water, for fish, for cheese. He runs to the door and he looks in. He is ravenous, so he does not stop. His friend the cat sit on the chair and she give him a look. Biscuit is ravenous, so he start to make a loud, funny sound. The cat laugh and say, "No, no, Biscuit, you need to sleep!" But Biscuit run to the table and he find a big red box. He open the box and there is a book, a blue hat, and some old food. Biscuit is ravenous, so he eat the food very, very quick. He look out the window and he see a small bird in the garden. He is ravenous for that bird too! Biscuit jump up, the cat run to help, and they play in the park all morning. The cat tell a new story to her friend, and Biscuit sit in the warm sun and read the book. They all feel happy, and Biscuit sleep, but not before he ask for more food.

The morning sun comes in through the window and hits the old book on the table.

The book is luminous. It shines so bright and warm that it makes the room feel happy.

A small cat sits on the chair and looks at the luminous book.

The cat wants to read the story in the book, but she is too hungry to sit and do that.

She walks to the table and her big green eyes look up at the luminous book.

Then a funny little mouse runs out from the box on the floor and into the garden.

The mouse is luminous too, like the sun is in its small body.

The cat and the mouse see each other and they start to laugh, or at least the cat does.

The cat is not sad now. She is happy to find a friend.

They sit close to the luminous window and look out at the quiet park.

The mouse tells the cat a new story, and the cat asks him to say it again.

They eat some cheese and fish and feel warm and good.

The sun goes down, and the luminous book on the table starts to go dark.

But the cat and the mouse do not want to stop. They want to play more.

They open the door, and the luminous night of the old sea is out there for them to see.

The night is quiet. Too quiet. I hear a small bird in the tree. This bird is nocturnal. It is out and active at night, not day. The nocturnal bird jumps and flies in the dark garden. I want to find it, but the garden is too dark. I go to the window and look out. There is the nocturnal bird, small and blue, close to the old tree. I call to it, but it does not come. Then I hear a new sound. A big cat is out in the street, and she is hungry. The nocturnal bird is quick. It runs up into the tree and hides. The cat sits on the wall and looks up, but she cannot find it. I think the cat is sad now. I go to her and give her some food. She is happy again. We sit in the quiet night and look at the tree. The nocturnal bird is there, warm and good. I tell my friend, and she says her old cat is like this too. We laugh, and the quiet night feels funny and kind.

The lake is very quiet. So quiet that you can hear the water and the birds and the rain all at one. It is tranquil, a warm and calm good morning for all the birds and the fish and the trees. A small cat sits by the water and she too feels tranquil, her big green eyes closed. She does not run. She does not jump. She is very still. A girl walks to the lake and she sees the cat. She is happy to find a quiet place to sit and read her book. The girl asks the cat, "Do you like this tranquil water?" The cat does not have a name, but the girl gives her one: Blue. Blue and the girl sit by the window of the old house and they read and eat some cheese and feel warm and good. A dog comes to the door and he is hungry and sad. Blue and the girl want to help him, so they open a box of food and give him some. The dog is happy now, and the two friends laugh. They walk out to the park, and the sun is warm and the rain is not here. The day is tranquil again, slow and kind. The girl thinks of a new story to tell her friend, and she smiles. She knows that a tranquil day like this is a good day to find a new word too.

The sun is warm and the rain is not here. A small dog and a boy walk out of the house and into the garden.

The dog does not run fast. He is slow, and he walks in a funny way. He meanders from the wall to the tree and back again.

The boy laugh and say, "You slow dog!"

They meander through the green garden, and the dog finds a big old chair and sits on it.

The boy is hungry, so he open a box of food and give some to the dog. The dog is very happy.

Then they hear a bird in the tree, and the boy ask the dog to come and look. But the dog meanders to the water and does not come.

The boy smile and say, "You slow dog, you do not help me!"

They sit and read a book in the warm sun, and the dog sleep on the floor of the garden.

At night, the boy look out the window and see the garden again. The rain is cold, but the dog is not here.

The boy think of the good day they meandered in the garden, and he feel happy.

The cat is very hungry, and she is sneaky. She is furtive, moving so quick and quiet that you do not hear her at all. She is furtive when she walks, and she is furtive when she sits, always close to the food but not seen. The dog does not know where the cat is, for the cat is too furtive to be found. The cat is furtive like a mouse in the night, small and quick, hiding behind the chair and then out again. She sees the big fish in the water, and she is sneaky to get it. The dog runs to the window to look, but the cat is already furtive and not there. The cat jumps up to the table, and there is the fish! She is sneaky and happy, and she does not stop to ask or say a word. The dog is sad and cold, but then he finds some food too, and he is not sad now. They sit and eat, and the cat gives him a funny look. The cat is sneaky, yes, but she is a good friend. They laugh, and the sun is warm in the garden.

The sky turned dark and the clouds came close. It was an ominous look, like the sky said "bad day here". The sun did not come out, and the rain started to fall. A big cat sat on the wall and looked at the garden. The cat was not happy, and her eyes were very big and sad. A dog ran to the door and asked for water. Then a mouse ran out from the old box near the floor. The mouse was quick, but the cat was quick too, and they jumped and ran all day. The cat and the mouse do not play now. The day was so ominous that even the bird in the tree did not sing. A friend came to the window and said, "I think we need to go home". We find the key to the old house, and we open the door. Inside, the room is warm and the chair is new and good. We sit down, and the dog and the cat come in too. The night is quiet, and the ominous sky is not here now. We read a book, we eat some food, and we all sleep. It is a very good home.

The boy is very hungry in the morning.

He looks at the table and sees a big, round fish on it.

He is voracious. He eats all the fish very quick and then asks for more.

His friend sees him and laughs, but she too is hungry.

The voracious boy runs to the door and calls out to his dog.

The dog comes in and they sit at the table, two voracious hungry friends.

They find a small box of cheese and eat all of it too.

Now the boy feels happy, but his cat is not happy. She is sad and cold.

The cat looks at them with big, green eyes from the chair.

The boy and his friend give the cat some food, and she is happy again.

They go out to the park, and the voracious boy runs and jumps like a funny bird.

His friend says, "You love to run and eat, do you not?"

They sit by the water and read a good story in the warm sun.

At home, the voracious boy opens a book and wants to read it all night.

His kind friend says, "Come, let us sleep here too."

They close the door, and the voracious boy smiles. He is very, very happy.

I find a small, old bone in the garden. It is a fossil, a very old bone that the rain and the sun did not destroy. I ask my friend to come and look at it too. She is happy to see it, and we sit close to the wall to read the story in the book. The book tell us that this fossil is from a big, funny bird that lived long ago. We dig in the cold, dry ground and find more. Two small fish bones, and one big red rock. I think the fossil and the old water on the floor make the room feel very slow and quiet. The cat in the house can not come out, so we laugh and play with the fossil all morning. Then the dog runs in and we all need to stop and help him get to his food. We open the box and give him some good, warm cheese. He is so hungry, and he is very happy now. I tell him his name is Fossil, and he sits down on the chair and we all smile. At night, I look out the window and see the blue sky, and I feel like a little old bird too.

There is a small hedgehog in the garden. He is very funny and very round. The hedgehog has a big red hat and a blue book. One day, the hedgehog finds a key on the floor. He runs to his friend, a cat, and says, "Come, help me!" The cat is a hedgehog too, or so it looks. They open a box and find some cheese inside. The hedgehog is so happy that he starts to eat and eat. But then he stop, for the cheese is cold and not good. He look at the cat and say, "No, no, this is bad." They go to the park and sit by a warm tree. There, a bird tell them a new story. The story is about a little hedgehog who love cheese and a kind dog who give him more. The hedgehog hear this and smile. He think, "Yes, I want a friend like that." Now he feel warm and good. He go home, sleep in his old chair, and dream of a new day.

The girl find an old box in the garden. She open it and see a small blue key. The key start to shimmer, like the sun on water. She hold it close and the key shimmer again, and she feel happy. She walk to her house and put the key in a box on the table. The next morning, she look at the box and the key shimmer again, so bright and warm. She ask her friend, "Do you see that? The key shimmer in the sun!" Her friend come to the house and they look at the key. It shimmer one more time, and then two new friends run into the room too. They all sit down and read a old book together. The book tell a story of a cat, a dog, and a very hungry mouse. The mouse read the story and laugh, for the mouse like the cat and the dog very much. Then the mouse say, "I need some food, and I need a friend." The girl give him cheese and a red hat, and the mouse eat and feel good. Now the key in the box shimmer again, and all of them smile. They know this good key help them find new friends every day.

The road to the park is serpentine. It bends and bends and does not go straight at all.

A car drive on the serpentine road, and it is very slow.

The serpentine road goes up, then down, then up again, like a funny old snake.

A dog run on the serpentine path, and he is very hungry.

He find a big red box and he want to open it.

A cat sit close to the box and she do not help him.

The dog ask his friend, "Do you know where the key is?"

His friend say, "No, but we can look in the old house there."

They go into the old house, and the room is cold and quiet.

The serpentine wall in this room go all the way to the top, and the dog need to climb it.

He climb up the wall, and then he see the key on the table.

He get the key, and they open the box. And there is good food in it!

The dog eat the food and he feel happy and warm.

His friend laugh and say, "You love this serpentine road!"

The dog smile and say, "Yes, I like it when it bend and bend and bend."

The old cat sat in the big room, and she did not move. She looked very forlorn, like a small bird with no home and no food. Her eyes were down, and her tail was close to the floor. A girl came to the door and said, "Do you need help?" The cat did not come to her, and the girl felt sad too. She was forlorn, like the cat, on a cold morning with no friend to play with. She looked out the window at the green garden, but the sun was not warm there anymore. She went to the table and found an old book and started to read. Then she heard a funny sound. A mouse ran out of a box and into the room! The forlorn cat jumped up quick, but the mouse was too quick for her. The girl laughed, and the cat looked at her with big, blue eyes. "You see," the girl said, "a little sad is good, but a big, lost, forlorn cat is not." They sit and eat some cheese and fish, and the cat gives the girl a warm, kind look. Now the room is not cold and sad. It is a new, happy home.

The morning sun is good and warm, but then the cold rain starts to come down. The rain is not the bad kind. It is the kind that makes the garden green and happy. But then the snow comes too, and the snow is big and cold. A blizzard is when the snow is so big and loud and cold that you cannot see or hear or run or play outside. The blizzard is so big that the whole town has to close the door to the street. The cat and the dog do not want to go out into the blizzard, so they sit in the warm room with the old friend. The cat and the dog look at the window and see the blizzard make the tree look like a funny blue hat. The dog asks the cat to open the door, but the cat says no. The cat is kind and she gives the dog some food and water. The dog is hungry, but he is slow and cold, so the cat has to help him. They find a small box and put the food in it, and the dog is happy again. The blizzard is not here now. The sun is warm and the garden is good. The cat and the dog sleep, and the old friend reads a new story to them. They do not need to work or run or play. They know this is a good night.

The old house on the green street has a big cellar. A room down very, very deep, under all the floor and wall. No one knows who made the cellar, and no one wants to go in. A small girl with a red hat walks up to the door of the cellar and looks in. She sees a funny old chair in the dark room, and she feels a little afraid. She asks her friend for help, and her friend gives her a key. The key is cold in her hand, and she has to think. She and her friend open the door, and they find old food there. Cheese, fish, and some blue and green and red boxes. The smell is not good and not bad, but the girl wants to know more. They sit down in the cold, quiet cellar and read an old book they find on the table. A small cat and a dog come to the window and look in at them. The girl is happy now, and she tells her friend a new story for them to laugh and smile. They close the door and go home, but the cat and the dog do not stop. They sleep in the warm room, and the girl and her friend laugh, for the cat and the dog like the cold, quiet cellar very much.

The girl and her dog go to the park one morning.

A small bird sits in the old tree and calls out a riddle. A funny question that needs a good answer.

The riddle is: "I have keys but no hands. I open doors but do not need a key. What am I?"

The dog looks up at the bird and then looks at the girl.

The girl thinks the riddle is not bad. She is happy to hear it again.

She starts to read a book she find on the floor of the park, and in it there is a new riddle about a cat and a fish.

The cat wants to catch the fish, but the fish is very quick.

The girl and the dog laugh at this riddle, and the bird in the tree smiles too.

Then the rain comes down, and they all run into the house at the end of the street.

The bird is cold and sad, so the girl gives him some food and a warm hat.

She tells him a kind story: "I love riddles. They help me think and find new words."

The bird and the girl sit by the window and read an old book together.

At night, the bird is happy, and he calls out one more riddle to them. A big, loud, funny riddle.

The girl and the dog laugh again, and the riddle is the best work they do all day.

The old boat sits in the green garden. A big cat sits on the boat, and the cat has a funny hat on her head. The cat does not run. She does not jump. She is very steady. A small dog runs to the boat and asks the cat to play. The cat is steady, so she does not jump at him. She just smiles and says, "Come, friend, we sit and look." They sit at the old table on the boat, and the cat gives the dog some food. The dog is hungry, and he is happy. Then the rain starts to come down, loud and cold. The cat is steady, and she does not hide. She holds her hand out to the water and says, "I love this rain." The dog look up at her and feel good again. The cat is like an anchor. She keeps them safe and calm in the storm. The dog think of the cat as a kind anchor, a friend he can trust. They sit and read a old book by the window, and the sun comes back into the room. The cat is steady, and the dog is steady too, and they sleep warm and quiet on the boat.

The night was cold and the rain came down. A big fire started in the old house. It was a blaze of red and blue light, so bright it could be seen from the park. The fire did not stop. It grew and grew, and the whole night was warm and loud. A dog ran out of his home to find his friend, but the rain was very cold. The fire made a blaze of light on the wall of the garden. A cat sat in a box and looked at the blaze with one eye open. The dog and the cat were hungry, so they found some food in the old room. A small mouse ran quick and quiet to the door. The mouse had a key in her hand, and she said, "Come, I have a new home here." They all walk in, and the fire blaze is now far away. The dog, the cat, and the mouse sit close and eat good food. They read a new book and feel happy. The rain stop, and the sun come out in the morning. It is a very good story.

The girl find a small pebble by the door of her house.

She pick it up and look at it. It is very round and smooth.

She put the pebble in her pocket and walk to the park.

A dog run to the park too, and he find a big, funny pebble on the floor.

The dog want to catch the girl, but she is too quick.

She throw the small pebble at the dog, and he jump and miss.

The dog laugh. Or he feel bad, but his eye is happy.

The girl and the dog sit by the water and see a bird in a tree.

The bird have a red hat and a blue and green name.

The girl tell a story to the dog, and the dog like the new word: pebble.

He know that pebble is small and he love it.

They go home, and the girl open her book and read by the window.

The dog sit on the floor and sleep, but he hear her. He come to the room.

She give him some food, and he is very happy.

The girl smile and say, "Yes, we find good work here, friend."

They sleep in the warm room, and the small pebble in her pocket feel warm too.

The river is very big and very wide. It goes through a gorge, a deep and narrow place between two big rocks. The boy and his dog walk along the gorge and look down at the water. The water is cold and the boy wants to jump in, but his dog says no. The dog is funny, and the boy laughs. They find a old chair at the edge of the gorge and sit down. The dog is hungry, so the boy gives him some food. Then they see a cat hide in the green tree close to the wall of the gorge. A bird is up in the tree too, and it looks at the cat. The cat and the bird look at one another, and the cat starts to feel sad. The boy and the dog want to help, but they do not know the cat. The dog runs to the cat with a small red box, and the cat jumps up and the bird flies out. The boy laughs again, and the dog and the cat sit down and eat cheese and feel warm. It is a good day in the gorge, and the boy is very happy. He tells his friend a story of the cat and the bird in his book, and his friend reads it at home. The gorge is a great place to play, and the boy and his dog go there again in the morning.

The old barn stood at the edge of the farm, big and quiet and green.

A small cat and a dog live in that barn now.

The cat likes to hide in the old box on top of the wall.

The dog runs to the door and calls out, but the cat is slow to come down.

One day, a bird comes to the window of the barn and asks for food.

The dog and the cat see the bird and feel happy.

They find some cheese and fish and give them to the bird.

The bird is very funny and she eat all the food very quick.

Then the rain starts to come down, loud and cold, on the barn.

The cat and the dog run to the door and close it, but the rain is too big.

They sit in the warm room and read a old book they find on the table.

The cat and the dog know they need to get more food for the bird.

So they go out into the garden and get more food for the bird.

The bird is happy here now, and the cat and the dog smile too.

The old barn is a good home for all of them.

The rain stops and the sun comes out. I walk to the garden and see a big meadow of green grass and flowers. The meadow is warm and good. I sit down on a chair and close my eyes. A small bird sits in the tree and looks at me. I feel happy now. In the morning, I find a red box by the door. I open it and see a funny old hat. I put the hat on and walk out to the meadow. The hat is blue and red, and it makes me look like a funny bird. A dog runs up to me and I laugh. Then I see a light in the far wall. It is a beacon, a bright, kind light that calls out to all who need help. I walk close to the beacon and it gives me warm light. I sit down in the meadow and read a book. The beacon is there, and I feel good. I tell the dog his name is Blue. We play and eat some food by the water. The meadow is home to us now. I think of the beacon and I smile. It is a good day.

The old house on the green street has a big, dark cellar. You hear a loud click and see a small red lantern come up from the cold, dark room.

A boy finds a key at the door of the cellar, and he opens it. The lantern in his hand is old and blue, but it gives out a warm light.

He looks down into the cellar and sees a funny cat sit on a chair. The cat has a hat on and a big smile. Or it looks like that, the boy think.

The boy and the cat start to play in the cold room, and the old lantern makes the whole dark cellar feel warm and good.

Then a bird comes to the window and calls out loud, and the cat jumps up quick. The boy laugh and say, "You like to hide!"

He and the cat find a box of old food in the cellar, and the boy is very hungry. He wants to eat some cheese and fish, but the cat wants to eat too.

The boy and the cat sit close, and the old lantern light up the room again. They read a story from a book they find on the table, and they feel happy and kind.

At night, when the rain comes down on the garden, the boy and the cat go to sleep in the warm, quiet cellar. And they do not need to go out into the cold, bad day.

The old boat sits in the harbor, close to the green water.

Every morning, a cat and a dog go out to the harbor to look at the sea.

The cat likes to sit on the old chair by the door of the small house in the harbor.

One day, the dog asks the cat: "Do you want to start a voyage? To go out into the big sea!"

A voyage is a long, slow journey on the water, and the cat is not very hungry for a voyage.

But the dog has a funny book, and in the book there is a story of a voyage to a new harbor.

They read the story again and again, and then the cat says, "Yes, let us go on a voyage!"

The boat is cold and the sun is out, and they put some food in a box to eat on the voyage.

They do not stop. They do not sleep. They do not hide. They run and jump and play all day.

At night, they hear a loud bird close to the wall of the harbor, and they feel very happy.

The voyage is long, but it is good, and they find a small, kind old fish in the water to help them.

They come back to the harbor, and the cat gives the fish some food and a red hat.

Now the dog and the cat sit in the warm room, and they read the funny book one more time.

The boy walk to the park one morning and see a big garden. In the garden, there is an old orchard. A place with very old trees that all have apples. The boy run to the orchard and look up at all the green trees. He feel ravenous. So hungry that he want to eat now, not play or sleep. He find a small red apple and eat it, but then he feel very hungry again. He ask his friend for more food, and she give him a box of cheese and fish. He is so ravenous that he eat all the cheese quick and then stop. The boy and his friend sit in the orchard and read a old book. In the book, they find a funny story about a fossil. A very old bone from a bird that live long ago. The boy look at the fossil and feel sad, but then he laugh. He tell his friend, "This fossil and this orchard. One good home for all of us." They sit and smile, and the sun go down behind the old trees.
